query_normalized: asymptotic behavior of random matrix eigenvalues with sparse perturbations mathematics
rationale: The paper focuses on algorithmic complexity for solving sparse linear systems and only mentions eigenvalue gaps as a technical tool for numerical stability analysis, rather than studying the asymptotic behavior of eigenvalues under sparse perturbations as a primary mathematical object. It fails to satisfy the criteria for same-mechanism evidence or domain overlap because its core contribution is computational speedup, not the spectral theory of perturbed random matrices.
